title: Chilam Balam of Chumayel: The Creation of the World
description: Mayan prophetic and cosmological texts: the creation and ordering of the world through the four cardinal directions.
introduction: The Books of Chilam Balam are the most important surviving texts of Yucatec Maya literature — prophetic histories, cosmological narratives, and ritual calendars transcribed in the Latin alphabet after the Spanish conquest but preserving pre-Columbian knowledge. The Chumayel version contains the most complete Maya creation account outside the Popol Vuh.

translation.original_text: He uah u tzolan katun, uaxac Ahau u katunil; hunac ceel u kinil.
translation.text: This is the record of the katun. Eight Ahau was the katun when it occurred; the first day was its day.

translation.original_text: Bay ix u hoppol cab yetel caan, tu lacal ba tumenel ti Dios.
translation.text: Thus was the beginning of earth and heaven, all things by the word of God.
